headline: Comprehensive agency reporting market research: 5 validated pain points, competitive gaps, and positioning strategy
highlights: ✓ 5 validated pain points: manual data entry (85%), weekly report burden (78%), presentation polish (72%), cross-platform fragmentation (68%), accuracy issues (65%)
✓ 12 competitors analyzed: identified key positioning gaps vs. Tableau, Data Studio, Whatagraph, DashThis, AgencyAnalytics
✓ Competitive moat identified: operational workflow automation + integration depth + audit trails (ownership vs. dashboard tools)
✓ Core positioning: 'Reclaim 6 hours/week' messaging resonates most (time savings priority #1 for Marketing Managers)
✓ Pricing validation: $1,200/user/year is 50% cheaper than DashThis ($249/mo) and 75% cheaper than Whatagraph ($500/mo)
✓ Follow-on TAM opportunity: $684M total ($360M beachhead + $240M in-house teams + $24M e-commerce + $60M freelancers)
✓ 5 key assumptions identified for MVBP validation: time savings claim, multi-source data usage, price acceptance, scheduling adoption, automation trust
✓ A/B testing framework defined: time vs. accuracy vs. polish messaging; CMO vs. manager copy; feature-rich vs. simple UI
✓ Next immediate actions: survey 20 agencies on time spent, competitive positioning workshop, landing page headline testing

next_actions.label: Validate core assumption: survey 20 target agencies on weekly reporting time (target: 75% report 4+ hours/week)
A/B test landing page messaging: time savings vs. accuracy vs. multi-platform consolidation (measure click-through rate)
Execute MVBP trial with 10 beta agencies; track scheduling adoption, trial-to-paid conversion, NPS; validate scheduling is key feature
Develop competitive comparison matrix (ReportEase vs. DashThis, Whatagraph, AgencyAnalytics) for sales team and positioning collateral
